rag_rules: Use valid AGREE constructs only inside an annex agree block.
In implementation scope, use assign only when the left side is an existing output feature or declared auxiliary variable.
AGREE expressions may reference only identifiers visible in the owning component scope.
generic_rules: Prefer implementation-level statements only for implementation-visible behavior; keep type-level relational guarantees separate from implementation-only details.
Write contracts from model-provided names and relations only; do not invent identifiers, paths, bounds, or constants.
Use concrete case-supplied relations only when they are expressible with visible symbols.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: Valid statement forms include assume, guarantee, eq, const, and assign, each ending with a semicolon.
Do not use implementation-only subcomponent paths inside a component type contract.
anti_patterns: Do not place guarantee statements in implementation scope when the intended pattern is an implementation assignment.
Do not reference AADL property names or unit tokens as if they were AGREE variables.
Do not copy example identifiers or use unresolved names outside current scope.
irrelevant_topics: AADL structural extraction details owned by the model analyst
Requirement classification or traceability decisions outside AGREE syntax generation
